jokes.text: D'oh! Ah!
jokes.setup: Opening sequence couch gag or mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific but predictable

jokes.text: Hold your horses. I was up all night fishing out the drawstring...from your father's swimsuit. It came out again.
jokes.setup: Family preparing for beach trip
jokes.punchline: Homer's swimsuit drawstring perpetually comes out, requiring Marge's late-night repairs
jokes.explanation: Relatable domestic humor with good character dynamics - Marge's dutiful frustration with Homer's incompetence

jokes.text: Well, I'm all set. I'm gonna be following the latest designs...from Modern Sandcastle Magazine.
jokes.setup: Lisa preparing for beach activities
jokes.punchline: The overly specific and pretentious 'Modern Sandcastle Magazine'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character comedy - her intellectualism applied to childhood activities, creating absurd specificity

jokes.text: I think she needs a diaper change. Well, it ain't happening today.
jokes.setup: Marge noting Maggie needs care
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt refusal to do any parenting on beach day
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: For each year, on the third Tuesday of May sweeps...we witness the return of the stinging red jellyfish.
jokes.setup: Treating jellyfish return as major news event
jokes.punchline: The meta reference to 'May sweeps' - TV ratings period
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on TV scheduling, breaking fourth wall subtly while maintaining story

jokes.text: What a sight. It happens every year...like the swallows returning to Capistrano. Or the Fox network getting a new president.
jokes.setup: Comparing jellyfish return to natural phenomena
jokes.punchline: Fox network's frequent executive turnover as equally predictable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecating humor about Fox's instability, delivered through elegant comparison structure

jokes.text: I made a new friend. [RALPH YELLS THEN GROANS]
jokes.setup: Ralph innocently announcing friendship
jokes.punchline: Immediate painful consequence, presumably jellyfish sting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum moment - naive optimism immediately punished by reality

jokes.text: I'm going to Africa...to see lions and giraffes and monkeys and Santa and gorillas and ...
jokes.setup: Ralph listing African animals he'll see
jokes.punchline: Santa casually inserted among actual African wildlife
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph logic - child's confusion about geography and Christmas mythology perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Now we know it just makes things a lot worse. - Like laser eye surgery? - Exactly.
jokes.setup: Debunking jellyfish venom as cure
jokes.punchline: Laser eye surgery as parallel example of harmful 'improvement'
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about medical procedures, well-structured comparison

jokes.text: Maybe we can have this band play at our wedding. I was hoping we could use this audio cassette.
jokes.setup: Skinner suggesting live band for wedding
jokes.punchline: Agnes's cheap alternative of a cassette tape
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Skinner's attempt at romance vs Agnes's penny-pinching nature

jokes.text: Sarah, you're as lovely as the day I first arrested you. Oh, Clancy, ha, ha. You know, I planted that crystal meth just to meet you. I was so shy.
jokes.setup: Romantic reminiscence about their first meeting
jokes.punchline: Wiggum planted evidence to create excuse to meet her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - police corruption framed as shy romance, perfectly fits Wiggum's character

jokes.text: Are you hitting on me? Because I don't do that...on land.
jokes.setup: Moe approaching Sea Captain at romantic festival
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain's maritime-specific sexual preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sea Captain character moment - his maritime obsession applied to sexuality with perfect timing

jokes.text: Well, guess I might as well head back to my store. I got a date with some twins, the state and federal tax forms.
jokes.setup: Moe leaving romantic festival alone
jokes.punchline: Tax forms as 'twins' he has a 'date' with
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Moe's sad-sack character and small business reality

jokes.text: There's the Tongue Kissing Festival, Cinco de Ocho, the Hobo Oscars. Days just made for lovers. Not widowers, lovers.
jokes.setup: Listing upcoming romantic events Ned will struggle with
jokes.punchline: Absurd festival names and the blunt 'Not widowers, lovers'
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor with 'Cinco de Ocho' and 'Hobo Oscars', plus darkly funny clarification

jokes.text: Happily married to a dead woman.
jokes.setup: Filling out tax form marital status
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of being 'happily married' to someone dead
jokes.explanation: Darkly perfect summation of Ned's situation - economical, character-driven, emotionally complex

jokes.text: Open as a tomb on Easter.
jokes.setup: Responding if his store is open
jokes.punchline: Religious reference to Jesus's empty tomb as metaphor for business availability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ned-specific religious wordplay - perfectly integrates his faith with everyday conversation

jokes.text: I gave up on L.A. when those TV people made that poor nun fly. All those Puerto Ricans looking up her dress. That's not right.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he dislikes L.A.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific reference to 'The Flying Nun' with moral outrage
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Ned's prudish nature through TV criticism, slightly convoluted

jokes.text: You're the most important person in the world because you're my customer.
jokes.setup: Customer asking if he knows who she is
jokes.punchline: Ned's genuine customer service philosophy over celebrity recognition
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that establishes Ned's genuine nature vs Hollywood superficiality

jokes.text: I'm as real as the nose on your face. Yeah. Real. [CLANGS] Well, it looks good. And it sounds pretty too.
jokes.setup: Ned claiming he's 'real' as her nose
jokes.punchline: Her nose makes a metallic clang sound, revealing it's fake, Ned's polite response
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup with Ned's characteristically polite response to awkward discovery

jokes.text: A woman asking a man out? Well. Well, why not? And maybe I'll eat my steak with a spoon.
jokes.setup: Sara asking Ned on a date
jokes.punchline: Ned's old-fashioned shock leading to absurd rebellion
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Ned's traditional mindset through escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Call me. I'm staying at the Springfield Four Seasons. [WHISPERS] Ask for Zelda Fitzgerald.
jokes.setup: Sara giving Ned her hotel information
jokes.punchline: Using literary figure's name as pseudonym in Springfield
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about celebrity secrecy, literary reference feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: What the--? That's a pseu-diddilyeudonym.
jokes.setup: Realizing Zelda Fitzgerald is a fake name
jokes.punchline: Ned's signature 'diddily' speech pattern applied to 'pseudonym'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanderism - his speech quirk applied to sophisticated vocabulary

jokes.text: I've got a date with a movie star? Sure you do. And I'm going apple picking with Scooby-Doo.
jokes.setup: Ned claiming date with movie star
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic disbelief through equally absurd scenario
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic comeback with good parallel structure, nothing exceptional

jokes.text: Flanders. Can't a man just drink alone on a Wednesday morning?
jokes.setup: Ned interrupting Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission of weekday morning drinking
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer alcoholism humor, well-integrated with his character but predictable

jokes.text: Let me guess. Skinner's mother? She sure knows how to swing it.
jokes.setup: Homer guessing about Ned's lady troubles
jokes.punchline: Homer's sexual interest in elderly Agnes Skinner
jokes.explanation: Mildly disturbing Homer sexuality humor, fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Why would that Twinkie wanna go out with a Ding Dong like me? Flanders, I mix Twinkies and Ding Dongs all the time. In Europe, they call it a Dinkie.
jokes.setup: Ned using snack cakes as self-deprecating metaphor
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation and fake European fact
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and character dynamics - Ned's metaphor vs Homer's literal food obsession

jokes.text: I'm no Arthur Miller or Lyle Lovett.
jokes.setup: Ned expressing insecurity about dating celebrity
jokes.punchline: Pairing prestigious playwright with unconventional-looking musician
jokes.explanation: Clever pairing of Miller (talent) and Lovett (married Julia Roberts despite looks)

jokes.text: I'm no Arthur Miller or Lyle Lovett.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why he can't date a movie star
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to unlikely celebrity partners
jokes.explanation: Smart reference humor about unlikely celebrity couples, shows Ned's awareness

jokes.text: But then part of her died and she doesn't try any more. So we're all where we wanna be.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how relationships work
jokes.punchline: Dark revelation about Marge giving up on improving him
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny marriage advice that's both depressing and relatable

jokes.text: Oh, man, this is going right on eBay. I mean, my wall, which I will sell on eBay.
jokes.setup: Fan getting autograph from Sara
jokes.punchline: Honest admission followed by attempt to cover with equally mercenary plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing fan's complete lack of sincerity

jokes.text: Oh, oh, can I just push this plastic cast onto one of your boobs?
jokes.setup: Increasingly inappropriate fan requests
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate physical request
jokes.explanation: Crosses into crude territory, relies on shock value rather than wit

jokes.text: Rod says 'Marco.' And darn it if Todd doesn't say 'Polo' right back.
jokes.setup: Ned sharing stories about his kids
jokes.punchline: Mundane pool game presented as remarkable brotherly bond
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned parenting - finding wonder in completely ordinary childhood activity

jokes.text: All they wanna talk about is their Botox, their Tae Bo or their Xbox.
jokes.setup: Sara complaining about actor boyfriends
jokes.punchline: Three 'x' ending vanities creating rhythm
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with good rhythm, but fairly predictable Hollywood satire

jokes.text: Heaven's gate. I'm gonna be tasting you on my moustache for a week. Wanna try for a month?
jokes.setup: Ned's romantic excitement after kiss
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly specific and overshare-y romantic enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Ned's awkward romantic inexperience

jokes.text: Well, yeah. She's had more stars on her than Lisa's homework.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Sara's dating history
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'stars' - celebrities vs gold stars on homework
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre with good visual parallel, well-constructed

jokes.text: Hello. Yes, I'm Ned Flanders' close personal friend. That's right. Hot and heavy. They've never been happier. Oh, yeah, she does look pregnant.
jokes.setup: Homer fielding tabloid phone calls
jokes.punchline: Escalating lies culminating in pregnancy claim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's mischievous nature and love of chaos

jokes.text: You guys are jamming. Ah! Daddy, she swore.
jokes.setup: Sara using mild slang with religious kids
jokes.punchline: Todd's shock at 'jamming' as profanity
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - ultra-sheltered kids vs normal person speaking

jokes.text: Ha, ha, yup. We occupy that useless mass of land...between Los Angeles and New York called America.
jokes.setup: Sara noting there are still people like Ned
jokes.punchline: Ned's self-deprecating geographic description of middle America
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural commentary delivered through Ned's self-aware humility

jokes.text: Are you sleeping with Sara Sloane? - Is that moustache real? Do you plan to kill Sara like your last wife?
jokes.setup: Paparazzi confronting Ned
jokes.punchline: Escalating from relationship to appearance to murder accusation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing tabloid ruthlessness, dark reference to Maude's death

jokes.text: Your carpeted floor feels good on my toes.
jokes.setup: Sara hiding from paparazzi in Ned's house
jokes.punchline: Hollywood actress appreciating basic middle-class comfort
jokes.explanation: Mild fish-out-of-water humor, not particularly sharp or memorable

jokes.text: This movie is turning into Sponge Bob No Pants.
jokes.setup: Ned objecting to nudity in Sara's movie
jokes.punchline: Innocent SpongeBob reference twisted to sound sexual
jokes.explanation: Good Ned moment - innocent reference that accidentally sounds inappropriate

jokes.text: Katharine Hepburn never showed her breasts. - There's still time.
jokes.setup: Ned citing classy actress precedent
jokes.punchline: Director's morbid optimism about elderly/dead actress
jokes.explanation: Dark Hollywood humor with good timing and economy

jokes.text: All we gotta do is turn that woman into a fella...and that kiss into a game of Scrabble.
jokes.setup: Ned negotiating movie changes
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd demands to avoid any sexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Ned's prudishness, turning romance into board game

jokes.text: Thanks. I grew really close to the actress who played my daughter. I think she's in France or something.
jokes.setup: Responding to compliment about her movie
jokes.punchline: Vague dismissal of former co-star's fate
jokes.explanation: Mild Hollywood superficiality humor, underwhelming delivery and impact

jokes.text: I haven't felt this good since we stole the 2000 election. Hey, don't blame me. I voted for the green M&M.
jokes.setup: Wiggum's political confession
jokes.punchline: Lou's absurd voting choice
jokes.explanation: Good political satire paired with absurdist voting choice, well-balanced

jokes.text: Uh, number one: Sara, is it true you shoplifted a kayak?
jokes.setup: Moe asking tabloid questions
jokes.punchline: Absurdly large item to shoplift
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - kayak is hilariously impractical thing to shoplift

jokes.text: I'll have you know she pays full price for her watercraft.
jokes.setup: Defending Sara against kayak theft accusation
jokes.punchline: Ned's earnest defense treating absurd question seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned response - taking ridiculous question completely seriously

jokes.text: If tears could burst through my muscular ducts...I would cry like a baby who was just hit by a hammer.
jokes.setup: Rainier trying to express emotion
jokes.punchline: Overly complicated metaphor with disturbing imagery
jokes.explanation: Good Rainier character humor - action star struggling with emotions awkwardly

jokes.text: Oh, come on. No one misses a handshake that badly.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he hit Jon Lovitz
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse for assault
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rainier's violent tendencies disguised as accidents

jokes.text: Oh, come on. No one misses a handshake that badly.
jokes.setup: Sara saying Rainier beat up Jon Lovitz for presenting her an award
jokes.punchline: Rainier's weak justification for violence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rainier's violent jealousy with weak excuse

jokes.text: Exact-a-tically-tacally.
jokes.setup: Rainier agreeing with Sara
jokes.punchline: His signature mangled English
jokes.explanation: Standard Rainier Wolfcastle callback, character-specific but predictable

jokes.text: I won't even eat vegetables over 2 inches long.
jokes.setup: Ned insisting he's not gay
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific food avoidance rule with sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - Ned's innocent prudishness creating sexual subtext

jokes.text: I guess you'll be heading out on the first bus to the airport. If you ride with a friend, you can save 20 bucks.
jokes.setup: Ned discussing Sara leaving Springfield
jokes.punchline: Money-saving tip reveals his middle-class mindset vs her wealth
jokes.explanation: Good class difference humor showing Ned's practical nature vs Hollywood excess

jokes.text: Ned, I'm James L. Brooks. - Oh, can I call you Jim? James L. Brooks is good.
jokes.setup: Famous producer introducing himself to Ned
jokes.punchline: Brooks insisting on full formal name
jokes.explanation: Mild Hollywood ego humor, fairly predictable celebrity behavior

jokes.text: Hey, daddy baby. - We're movie producers now. - And we're Jewish. ROD & TODD: Yay! [SCREAMS]
jokes.setup: Rod and Todd excited about Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Kids' innocent excitement about religious conversion, Ned's horror
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy contrasting kids' innocence with Ned's religious anxiety

jokes.text: Heck, even Dollywood's too far out for me. Too many people appreciating it ironically.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why he won't move to Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Even Dolly Parton's theme park is too sophisticated/ironic for him
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Ned's discomfort with cultural irony, even in wholesome contexts

jokes.text: Problem is I can't seem to get an agent. Ha. Could you give your girlfriend my headshot?
jokes.setup: Officer pulling over Ned and Sara
jokes.punchline: Using police authority to pursue acting career
jokes.explanation: Standard 'everyone in LA wants to be an actor' humor, predictable

jokes.text: Sure is nice to be shopping for a woman again. Last thing I bought for a lady was a casket.
jokes.setup: Ned enjoying shopping for Sara
jokes.punchline: Dark reminder of buying Maude's casket
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy callback to Maude's death, perfectly integrated with character

jokes.text: Wait a minute. This is to adopt two of your kids. Oh, so it is. I've already dumped three on Mia Farrow. Sucker.
jokes.setup: Fan asking for autograph
jokes.punchline: Adoption papers disguised as autograph, reference to Farrow's many adoptions
jokes.explanation: Dark celebrity humor with good escalation, though character feels generic

jokes.text: Lisa, Springfield isn't a cultural wasteland. Part of Stroker Ace was filmed at our airport.
jokes.setup: Defending Springfield's culture
jokes.punchline: Citing terrible Burt Reynolds movie as cultural achievement
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference humor - terrible movie as point of local pride

jokes.text: Sara Sloane to attend girl-on-girl book club. Ho-ho, Moe, you lovable loser. You just made yourself $5. Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Moe misreporting book club as sexual
jokes.punchline: Homer's support for Moe's tabloid career
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo humor, relies on misunderstanding rather than wit

jokes.text: Now, let's go around the room and analyze why we didn't read it. - Cramps. - All my friends are dead.
jokes.setup: Book club analyzing why they didn't read book
jokes.punchline: Escalation from mild excuse to existential despair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor about book clubs that don't actually read

jokes.text: Besides, most Americans can't understand...the sophisticated subtlety of British humor. I bid you good day. [THEME FROM 'THE BENNY HILL SHOW' PLAYING]
jokes.setup: British author claiming sophisticated humor
jokes.punchline: Benny Hill theme immediately contradicting her claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic timing - pretentious claim immediately undercut by lowbrow music cue

jokes.text: Sweetie, that's not the kind of dress you wear outdoors or indoors...or in a dirty dream.
jokes.setup: Ned objecting to Sara's revealing dress
jokes.punchline: Escalation through all contexts including his own fantasies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ned's complete prudishness, even in private thoughts

jokes.text: Well, Siegfried and Roy create a lot of illusions...but I doubt their girlfriends dress like that.
jokes.setup: Sara mentioning illusion of nudity in dress
jokes.punchline: Ned's innocent reference to famously gay magicians' non-existent girlfriends
jokes.explanation: Good innocent Ned humor - missing obvious subtext about Siegfried and Roy

jokes.text: That's disgusting. Don't look, Seymour. - I'm a veteran, Mother. - A veteran bed wetter.
jokes.setup: Agnes shielding Skinner from Sara's dress
jokes.punchline: Skinner's dignity immediately undercut by embarrassing detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner/Agnes dynamic - his attempt at dignity crushed by maternal humiliation

jokes.text: [HOMER GRUNTING AND CHANTING] Ah. Ch! Ah. Ch! Ah. Ch! Ah.
jokes.setup: Homer making rhythmic sounds during concert
jokes.punchline: His crude participation in refined cultural event
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer at culture event humor, predictable fish-out-of-water comedy

jokes.text: Uh, well, that's, uh, a mighty big kettle of, uh...premarital, uh, doodly. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Sara wanting sex with Ned
jokes.punchline: Ned's complete inability to discuss sexuality using childish language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned sexual awkwardness, using nonsense words to avoid saying 'sex'

jokes.text: This is quite a dil-diddly-lemma.
jokes.setup: Ned contemplating sexual dilemma
jokes.punchline: His speech pattern applied to 'dilemma'
jokes.explanation: Classic Flanderism perfectly applied to situation, highly quotable

jokes.text: I mean, who will buy the cow when you get the milk for free? You know, the cow being me.
jokes.setup: Ned using traditional saying about premarital sex
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he's the cow in the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good gender reversal of traditional saying with Ned's earnest clarification

jokes.text: It says a man should make restitution if he eats his neighbor's grain.
jokes.setup: Ned finding random Bible verse to justify sex
jokes.punchline: Completely irrelevant agricultural law applied to romantic situation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about biblical justification for modern situations

jokes.text: That made me completely forget about Bob Balaban.
jokes.setup: Sara commenting on sex with Ned
jokes.punchline: Forgetting about obscure character actor rather than leading man
jokes.explanation: Funny reference to character actor Bob Balaban as unlikely romantic interest

jokes.text: That's right. I'm like Baskin-Robbins. You get one free taste, then you gotta buy the scoop.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining his marriage requirement
jokes.punchline: Ice cream metaphor for sexual relationship
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that fits Ned's wholesome nature while discussing sex

jokes.text: But unlike the Bible, I guess this ain't gonna have a happy ending.
jokes.setup: Realizing relationship won't work
jokes.punchline: Comparing relationship to Bible's narrative structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific metaphor - Ned using biblical narrative structure to understand romance

jokes.text: Oh, darn the luck. That sounds like the Lupus Fun Run.
jokes.setup: Hearing footsteps during breakup scene
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate cheerfulness about disease charity run
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor contrasting serious disease with 'fun run' concept

jokes.text: This was followed three hours later by a quickie divorce. Ah. I bet we would have lasted twice that long.
jokes.setup: News of Sara's quick marriage/divorce to Bob Balaban
jokes.punchline: Ned's modest claim of lasting six hours
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor showing Ned's realistic expectations

jokes.text: Hey, Hollywood, call me. My number's on the bench.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy seeking Hollywood attention
jokes.punchline: Advertising like a desperate prostitute
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor about Comic Book Guy's desperate self-promotion

jokes.text: It's the darnedest thing. Women love you when you've dated a movie star.
jokes.setup: Ned reflecting on his newfound dating success
jokes.punchline: Celebrity association as aphrodisiac
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observation about celebrity culture and dating, not particularly clever
